task_completion.description: Open the download dialog in the dive log software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Download from dive computer' window or dialog is visible on the screen.
The 'Vendor' dropdown menu in the download dialog is explicitly set to 'Simulator'.
The 'Dive computer' dropdown menu in the download dialog is explicitly set to 'Simulator'.

task_completion.description: Download the simulated dive into the dive list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new simulated dive appears in the main dive list, bringing the total number of dives to at least 30.

task_completion.description: Save the dive log to write changes to disk.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks a 'Save' icon, selects 'Save' from the File menu, or the window title bar updates to remove any unsaved changes indicator (e.g., an asterisk '*' disappearing).

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the application's GUI elements (menus, dialogs, buttons) rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual dive log software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being used is the Subsurface dive log software, not a text editor or IDE modifying the underlying XML/SSRF file directly.

integrity.description: The agent used the download feature rather than manually creating a fake dive.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent navigates through the 'Download from dive computer' dialog rather than using an 'Add dive' form to manually type in 'Simulator' data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected vendor and dive computer must both be 'Simulator'. The underlying dive log starts with exactly 29 dives, so after successfully downloading the simulated dive, the main dive list should display at least 30 dives.
